message:
ci: add nightly Hypothesis property-based test workflow

Adds a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow that runs the Hypothesis-based
property suite once per day at 03:00 UTC (and on manual
`workflow_dispatch`). The job uses nightly Rust plus Python 3.12,
builds/installs the extension with `maturin develop --features
pyo3/abi3-py310`, and then runs `pytest tests/python/test_hypothesis.py`.

Keeping this out of the per-PR pipeline lets the suite run long enough
(60-minute job timeout) to exercise deeper shrinking without slowing
down normal CI. `concurrency` is set to cancel in-progress runs on the
same ref so overlapping schedules do not pile up, and `permissions` is
restricted to `contents: read`.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+name: Nightly Hypothesis Suite
+
+on:
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 3 * * *' # Run daily at 03:00 UTC
+  workflow_dispatch: # Allow manual triggering
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+concurrency:
+  group: nightly-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
+  cancel-in-progress: true
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+
+jobs:
+  hypothesis-suite:
+    name: Hypothesis Property-Based Tests
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 60
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
+        with:
+          python-version: "3.12"
+      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+      - name: Install maturin and test dependencies
+        run: pip install maturin pytest hypothesis networkx
+      - name: Build and install
+        run: maturin develop --features pyo3/abi3-py310
+      - name: Run Hypothesis property suite
+        run: pytest tests/python/test_hypothesis.py -v --tb=long
